So each fix is: declare a `personID` (and, for\nautomation/explainability, the other identity fields) the same way, then set them on the target\nstruct literal.\n\n### 1.1 What `GetMatch` returns (the sidecar fields available to populate from)\n\n`ble.IdentityMatcher.GetMatch(blobID int) *IdentityMatch` (`internal/ble/identity.go:609`).\n`IdentityMatch` (`internal/ble/identity.go:34`) carries:\n\n| Field | Type | Use for |\n|---|---|---|\n| `PersonID` | `string` | the stable person key — **always populate this** (drives `personProvider.GetPerson`) |\n| `PersonName` | `string` | resolved label (e.g. \"Alice\") |\n| `PersonColor` | `string` | CSS hex color |\n| `DeviceAddr` | `string` | matched BLE device address |\n| `Confidence` | `float64` | match confidence [0..1] |\n| `TriangulationPos` | `Position{X,Y,Z}` | BLE RSSI centroid (used by explainability overlay) |\n\n> Note the field-name mapping the existing code already uses (main.go:2221-2230): the\n> explainability `identityMap` copies `match.PersonName → PersonLabel`. Keep that mapping\n> consistent when folding #2.\n\n---\n\n## 2. If\n * `tsc` exits 0, every assignment below type-checks against the `Blob`\n * interface in `./spaxel.d.ts`.\n *\n * Scope note: only the identity fields are in scope for bf-56uk. The\n * pre-existing `Blob.id: string` vs. the Go wire `id` (emitted as a JSON\n * number from `blobJSON.ID int`) is intentionally NOT asserted here — it is a\n * separate, non-identity concern tracked elsewhere.\n */\n\nimport type { Blob } from './spaxel';\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Type-level assertions: the identity fields are declared on `Blob` with the\n// exact names and types the Go `blobJSON` tags emit. If any name drifts, the\n// corresponding `AssertTrue<…>` line becomes a compile error.\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Compile-time \"true\" sentinel — only `true` satisfies it. */\ntype AssertTrue<T extends true> = T;\n\n/**\n * True iff `T` declares key `K` whose type accepts a value of type `V`\n * (i.e. `V extends T[K]`). Returns `false` when `K` is absent.\n *\n * Implementation note: this is deliberately written with the concrete `V`\n * (e.g. `string`, `boolean`) on the LEFT of `extends` and the indexed `T[K]`\n * on the RIGHT. The reverse shape — `NonNullable<T[K]> extends NonNullable<V>`\n * — is intentionally NOT used: TypeScript does not reliably evaluate a utility\n * type (`NonNullable`) applied to an *indexed type parameter* `T[K]` inside a\n * nested conditional, which previously produced a spurious `false` (TS2344)\n * for the optional `boolean` `identityResolved` field. `V extends T[K]`\n * resolves correctly in every case and still catches both a missing key\n * (`false`) and a type-mismatched field (`false`).\n */\ntype HasField<T, K extends PropertyKey, V> = K extends keyof T\n    ? V extends T[K]\n        ? true\n        : false\n    : false;\n\n// Canonical identity fields (bf-5151): camelCase JSON keys from hub.go blobJSON.\ntype _PersonNameIsString = AssertTrue<HasField<Blob, 'personName', string>>;\ntype _AssignedColorIsString = AssertTrue<HasField<Blob, 'assignedColor', string>>;\ntype _IdentityResolvedIsBool = AssertTrue<HasField<Blob, 'identityResolved', boolean>>;\n\n// Deprecated aliases retained on the interface for backward compatibility.\ntype _PersonLabelIsString = AssertTrue<HasField<Blob, 'personLabel', string>>;\ntype _PersonColorIsString = AssertTrue<HasField<Blob, 'personColor', string>>;\ntype _PersonIdIsString = AssertTrue<HasField<Blob, 'personId', string>>;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (1) Zero-value blob: identity fields omitted entirely. Must satisfy `Blob`\n//     with no missing-field errors. Mirrors the Go \"zero-value blob omits all\n//     three fields (undefined in JS)\" assertion.\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst zeroValueBlob: Blob = {\n    id: '1',\n    x: 0,\n    y: 0,\n    z: 0,\n    confidence: 0,\n};\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (2) Resolved-identity blob: carries the exact camelCase keys emitted by the\n//     Go dashboard projection (hub.go: personName / assignedColor /\n//     identityResolved). Must satisfy `Blob` with no type-mismatch errors.\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst resolvedIdentityBlob: Blob = {\n    id: '2',\n    x: 3.2,\n    y: 1.1,\n    z: 0.8,\n    confidence: 0.85,\n    vx: 0.3,\n    vy: -0.1,\n    vz: 0,\n    posture: 'walking',\n    personName: 'Alice',\n    assignedColor: '#4488ff',\n    identityResolved: true,\n};\n\n// Deprecated aliases still accepted by the interface (backward compat).\nconst legacyAliasBlob: Blob = {\n    id: '3',\n    x: 0,\n    y: 0,\n    z: 0,\n    confidence: 0,\n    personId: 'alice-uuid',\n    personLabel: 'Alice', // deprecated alias of personName\n    personColor: '#4488ff', // deprecated alias of assignedColor\n};\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (3) identityResolved tri-state, mirroring the Go `*bool` + `omitempty`\n//     contract: nil → omitted (undefined), &true → true, &false → false.\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst identityNotAttempted: Blob = {\n    ...zeroValueBlob,\n    id: '4',\n};\n\nconst identityResolvedTrue: Blob = {\n    ...zeroValueBlob,\n    id: '4a',\n    identityResolved: true,\n};\n\nconst identityResolvedFalse: Blob = {\n    ...zeroValueBlob,\n    id: '4b',\n    identityResolved: false,\n};\n\n// Explicit `undefined` is assignable (field present but unset).\nconst identityExplicitlyUndefined: Blob = {\n    ...zeroValueBlob,\n    id: '4c',\n    identityResolved: undefined,\n};\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Dashboard state map shape: appState.blobs is Record<string, Blob>\n// (dashboard/js/state.js: appState.blobs[id]). A mixed map of zero-value and\n// resolved blobs must satisfy it wholesale.\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst blobMap: Record<string, Blob> = {\n    [zeroValueBlob.id]: zeroValueBlob,\n    [resolvedIdentityBlob.id]: resolvedIdentityBlob,\n    [legacyAliasBlob.id]: legacyAliasBlob,\n    [identityResolvedFalse.id]: identityResolvedFalse,\n};\n\n// `Object.assign(target, updates)` in state.js merges a server blob into an\n// existing Blob entry; the updates shape must be assignable to a partial Blob.\nfunction mergeBlobUpdate(target: Blob, updates: Partial<Blob>): Blob {\n    return Object.assign(target, updates);\n}\n\nconst merged = mergeBlobUpdate(zeroValueBlob, {\n    personName: 'Bob',\n    assignedColor: '#ff8800',\n    identityResolved: true,\n});\n\n// Reference the bindings so they are not flagged as unused by tooling that\n// walks runtime ASTs (tsc itself needs no this under noEmit, but it keeps the\n// file honest for any future runtime pass).\nexport const __blobIdentityChecks = {\n    zeroValueBlob,\n    resolvedIdentityBlob,\n    legacyAliasBlob,\n    identityNotAttempted,\n    identityResolvedTrue,\n    identityResolvedFalse,\n    identityExplicitlyUndefined,\n    blobMap,\n    merged,\n};","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c6f3ad05-afc2-4087-a025-1216a6fcddb3","uuid":"e14975d0-0a1d-4fa7-b6af-3e08bb788e9d","timestamp":"2026-07-08T21:24:23.756Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"=== blob-identity.check.ts (full) ===\n/**\n * Compile-time verification that the blob identity fields are\n * TypeScript-compliant (bead bf-56uk).\n *\n * This file is the TypeScript mirror of the Go serialization suites added\n * across bf-5151 / bf-2ibc / bf-5v3q / bf-3wkz / bf-4qto:\n *   - mothership/internal/signal/identity_fields_test.go\n *   - mothership/internal/automation/identity_fields_test.go\n *   - mothership/internal/tracker/identity_fields_test.go\n *   - mothership/internal/tracking/identity_fields_test.go\n *   - mothership/internal/volume/identity_fields_test.go\n *   - mothership/internal/explainability/identity_fields_test.go\n *   - mothership/internal/api/tracks_identity_test.go\n *\n * It asserts the three task-required properties of bf-56uk:\n *   1. a zero-value blob (no identity set) satisfies `Blob` — no missing-field\n *      errors; {"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"message_stop"},"session_id":"c6f3ad05-afc2-4087-a025-1216a6fcddb3","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"596cb41f-71a3-4cc6-9150-02dbf63bab3a"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_8ac10b81425646c59e05a26a","type":"tool_result","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). A `sqlite3` binary was available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3` as of 2026-07-06 (not on PATH) — but **as of 2026-07-08 that Nix store path is GONE** (`find /nix/store -name sqlite3` returns nothing; it was garbage-collected). **`python3`'s built-in `sqlite3` module is now the primary repair method, not a fallback** — there is no sqlite3 binary on this box. Both RFC3339 suffixes are accepted by `br close`: the `+00:00` form above AND trailing `Z` via `strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', claimed_at)` (equivalently `REPLACE(ts,' ','T')||'Z'`) both worked. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time.
